title: Author a library target with correct visibility
description: Define a library, attach its includes and dependencies with the right PUBLIC/PRIVATE/INTERFACE scope, and verify a consumer links it cleanly.
body: Use when adding a new library component to a CMake project. Do not default every requirement to PUBLIC. Actions: add_library with its sources; target_include_directories using PUBLIC for headers consumers include and PRIVATE for internal ones; target_link_libraries with visibility matching each dependency's need; add a small consumer target and confirm it builds via link alone. Validation signals include the consumer compiling without restating includes and only intended requirements propagating. Risk boundary: leaking implementation dependencies through PUBLIC is hard to reverse once consumers depend on them, so scope deliberately.
